items.q: What does modulation mean in music?
items.correct: Changing from one key to another within a piece.
items.why: Students conflate modulation with any musical change. Specifically, modulation is a KEY CHANGE, usually established by a perfect cadence in the new key - a structural device.

items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).

items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.
The answer examiners want is: Trombone. “Trumpet” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Percussion. “Brass” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many common orchestral brass instruments are listed?
items.why: The real figure is 4. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
